segments.text: Bengaluru is the third slowest city on earth while Hyderabad is 18th. Let's understand why. See, Bengaluru grew explosively fast. But here is what exactly happened. Buildings came first,
roads came second. Private developers built on agricultural land and infrastructure was always playing catch-up. The result, Bangaluru now has
22 unfinished flyovers and they are spending over 27,000 cr just trying to fix what is broken. But Hyderabad also grew fast. Same tech boom, same
pressure. But in 2001, they made this one decision. Build outer ring road first before selling the land around it.

Finally, Hyderabad's outer ring road was completed in 2018. But developers still could not sell the land until the master plan was ready. This was their master plan. infrastructure waiting for people,
